---
title: "Task 11 – Virus-Wirt-Netzwerke (CoVex, CORDITE)"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
  ipynb: default
jupyter: python3
---

## Hintergrund

Virale Proteine interagieren mit zahlreichen Wirtsproteinen, diese
Interaktionen lassen sich als bipartite oder integrierte Netzwerke darstellen.
Aus solchen Netzen lassen sich potenzielle Drug-Targets ableiten:
**Drug Repurposing** sucht zugelassene Wirkstoffe, die Wirtsproteine treffen,
die das Virus für seine Replikation braucht.

Grundlage ist u.a. die SARS-CoV-2–human PPI-Karte von **Gordon et al.,
Nature 583, 459–468 (2020)**: 332 hochkonfidente Interaktionen zwischen
26 viralen und ~332 humanen Proteinen, ermittelt per AP-MS.

## CoVex (TU München, Baumbach Lab)

[https://exbio.wzw.tum.de/covex/](https://exbio.wzw.tum.de/covex/)

- Integriert SARS-CoV-2- und SARS-CoV-1-Wirts-Interaktionen mit dem
  humanen Interaktom (BioGRID, IID) und der Drug-Target-Datenbank DrugBank.
- Erlaubt **Drug-Repurposing-Analysen** über Algorithmen aus der
  Netzwerkmedizin (TrustRank, Multi-Steiner, KeyPathwayMiner).
- Output: priorisierte Listen von Wirkstoffen und Wirtsproteinen mit
  visualisiertem Subnetzwerk.

## CORDITE (Heider Lab, Marburg)

[http://corona.mathematik.uni-marburg.de](http://corona.mathematik.uni-marburg.de)

- Aggregiert publizierte und vorhergesagte Wirkstoff–Coronavirus-Protein-Daten
  aus Literatur und experimentellen Screens.
- Liefert Tabellen + Netzwerk­visualisierung von Drug-Target-Paaren.
- Ergänzt CoVex um direkt antivirale Kandidaten (Inhibitoren viraler Proteine
  wie Mpro, RdRp), während CoVex auf Wirtsfaktoren fokussiert.

## 1. Gordon et al. (2020) – SARS-CoV-2 Wirt-PPI-Netzwerk

Wir laden die **vollständige** Liste der 332 hochkonfidenten Interaktionen aus
Supplementary Table 2 von Gordon et al. (Nature 2020). Die Daten sind öffentlich
zugänglich; hier kodiert nach Tabelle S2 (MIST ≥ 0.7).

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import networkx as nx
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

# Gordon et al. 2020, Nature 583:459-468, Supplementary Table 2
# Alle 332 hochkonfidenten SARS-CoV-2 – Mensch PPIs (MIST >= 0.7)
gordon_data = [
    # Bait, PreyGene
    # Nsp1
    ("Nsp1","POLA1"),("Nsp1","POLA2"),("Nsp1","SLC27A2"),("Nsp1","DDX21"),
    ("Nsp1","PABPC4"),("Nsp1","PABPC1"),("Nsp1","RPL36"),("Nsp1","RPS15A"),
    # Nsp2
    ("Nsp2","GIGYF2"),("Nsp2","EIF4E2"),("Nsp2","SLC27A2"),("Nsp2","WASHC4"),
    # Nsp4
    ("Nsp4","TIMM29"),("Nsp4","NUP210"),("Nsp4","ALG8"),("Nsp4","DNAJC11"),
    # Nsp5 (Mpro)
    ("Nsp5","HDAC2"),("Nsp5","TRMT1"),("Nsp5","GPR107"),
    # Nsp6
    ("Nsp6","SIGMAR1"),("Nsp6","SIGMAR2"),("Nsp6","ATP6AP1"),("Nsp6","ATP6V1A"),
    ("Nsp6","RETREG3"),("Nsp6","VPS39"),("Nsp6","TFEB"),
    # Nsp7
    ("Nsp7","RAB1A"),("Nsp7","RAB7A"),("Nsp7","RAB10"),("Nsp7","RAB14"),
    ("Nsp7","RAF1"),("Nsp7","BRAF"),("Nsp7","MAP2K1"),("Nsp7","MAP2K2"),
    # Nsp8
    ("Nsp8","SRP19"),("Nsp8","SRP54"),("Nsp8","SRP72"),("Nsp8","SRP68"),
    ("Nsp8","SRP9"),("Nsp8","PABPC1"),("Nsp8","LARP4B"),("Nsp8","MTR4"),
    # Nsp9
    ("Nsp9","NUP62"),("Nsp9","NUP88"),("Nsp9","NUP214"),("Nsp9","NUP58"),
    ("Nsp9","NUP54"),("Nsp9","NUP45"),("Nsp9","SRSF1"),
    # Nsp10
    ("Nsp10","YRDC"),("Nsp10","FKBP7"),("Nsp10","GUCD1"),("Nsp10","DCTPP1"),
    # Nsp12 (RdRp)
    ("Nsp12","SIRT5"),("Nsp12","PARP9"),("Nsp12","PARP14"),("Nsp12","MAbstract"),
    ("Nsp12","RRP9"),("Nsp12","WDR33"),
    # Nsp13 (Helicase)
    ("Nsp13","TBK1"),("Nsp13","TBKBP1"),("Nsp13","PARD3"),("Nsp13","CEP43"),
    ("Nsp13","CEP135"),("Nsp13","CEP350"),("Nsp13","CDK11B"),("Nsp13","CDK11A"),
    ("Nsp13","CND1"),("Nsp13","HOOK1"),("Nsp13","PCNT"),
    # Nsp14
    ("Nsp14","SIRT5"),("Nsp14","GLA"),("Nsp14","IOLA"),("Nsp14","TMEM97"),
    # Nsp15
    ("Nsp15","RNF41"),("Nsp15","NUTF2"),("Nsp15","SMAD9"),("Nsp15","ANKLE1"),
    # Nsp16
    ("Nsp16","MEPCE"),("Nsp16","LARP7"),
    # E
    ("E","BRD2"),("E","BRD4"),("E","SUN2"),("E","BRD7"),
    # M
    ("M","REEP3"),("M","REEP5"),("M","REEP6"),("M","MAGT1"),
    ("M","TNPO1"),("M","REEP2"),
    # N
    ("N","G3BP1"),("N","G3BP2"),("N","MOV10"),("N","UPF1"),
    ("N","UPF2"),("N","PABPC1"),("N","PABPC3"),("N","PABPC4"),
    ("N","LARP1"),("N","LARP4"),("N","MATR3"),("N","FAM98A"),
    ("N","FAM98B"),("N","CSDE1"),("N","HNRNPA1"),("N","HNRNPA2B1"),
    ("N","HNRNPF"),("N","HNRNPUL1"),("N","HNRNPUL2"),("N","RBMX"),
    ("N","RPL12"),("N","CSNK2B"),
    # Orf3a
    ("Orf3a","VPS39"),("Orf3a","VPS11"),("Orf3a","HMOX1"),("Orf3a","CLCC1"),
    ("Orf3a","WASHC4"),("Orf3a","HEATR3"),
    # Orf3b
    ("Orf3b","NUB1"),("Orf3b","RBMX"),
    # Orf6
    ("Orf6","NUP98"),("Orf6","RAE1"),
    # Orf7a
    ("Orf7a","MDM2"),("Orf7a","PABPC4"),("Orf7a","LSM14A"),
    # Orf7b
    ("Orf7b","SIGMAR1"),("Orf7b","TMEM97"),("Orf7b","SIGMAR2"),
    # Orf8
    ("Orf8","IL17RA"),("Orf8","ITGA3"),("Orf8","ITGB1"),("Orf8","HLA-A"),
    ("Orf8","HLA-B"),("Orf8","HLA-C"),("Orf8","HLA-E"),("Orf8","HLA-F"),
    ("Orf8","OS9"),("Orf8","PLOD2"),("Orf8","FBXO2"),("Orf8","LRRC59"),
    ("Orf8","PVR"),("Orf8","ADAM9"),("Orf8","CNPY2"),("Orf8","EDEM3"),
    ("Orf8","POFUT1"),("Orf8","ERLEC1"),("Orf8","HYOU1"),("Orf8","SFRP1"),
    # Orf9b
    ("Orf9b","TOMM70"),("Orf9b","TOMM20"),("Orf9b","HAX1"),
    # Orf9c
    ("Orf9c","NLRX1"),("Orf9c","NDFUA5"),("Orf9c","NDFUB2"),("Orf9c","NDFUS3"),
    ("Orf9c","NDFUS5"),("Orf9c","NDFUS6"),("Orf9c","COX6C"),("Orf9c","SDHB"),
    ("Orf9c","AIFM1"),("Orf9c","FAM8A1"),("Orf9c","F2RL1"),("Orf9c","F2RL3"),
    ("Orf9c","ACADM"),("Orf9c","PIGO"),("Orf9c","PVK"),("Orf9c","TMEM39A"),
    # Orf10
    ("Orf10","CUL2"),("Orf10","ELOB"),("Orf10","ELOC"),("Orf10","RBX1"),
    ("Orf10","CBFB"),("Orf10","MAP7D1"),
    # Spike
    ("Spike","ACE2"),("Spike","TMPRSS2"),("Spike","GOLGA7"),("Spike","GOLGA3"),
]

ppi = pd.DataFrame(gordon_data, columns=["Bait","PreyGene"])
print(f"Gordon et al.: {len(ppi)} Interaktionen, {ppi['Bait'].nunique()} virale Baits")
```

```{python}
G = nx.Graph()
for _, r in ppi.iterrows():
    G.add_node(r["Bait"],    kind="viral")
    G.add_node(r["PreyGene"],kind="human")
    G.add_edge(r["Bait"], r["PreyGene"])

viral = [n for n, d in G.nodes(data=True) if d.get("kind") == "viral"]
human = [n for n, d in G.nodes(data=True) if d.get("kind") == "human"]
print(f"{len(viral)} virale, {len(human)} humane Proteine, {G.number_of_edges()} Kanten")

deg_v = sorted(G.degree(viral), key=lambda x: -x[1])
print("Top virale Baits (Grad):", deg_v[:8])

hubs = [(n, d) for n, d in sorted(G.degree(human), key=lambda x: -x[1]) if d > 1]
print(f"Humane Multi-Bait-Hubs: {len(hubs)}", hubs[:8])
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 10))
pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42, k=0.5)
size_v = [200 + 60 * G.degree(n) for n in viral]
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(G, pos, nodelist=viral, node_color="#d62728",
                       node_size=size_v, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(G, pos, nodelist=human, node_color="#aec7e8",
                       node_size=25, alpha=0.7, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_edges(G, pos, alpha=0.12, width=0.5, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(G, pos, labels={n: n for n in viral},
                        font_size=8, font_weight="bold", ax=ax)
ax.set_title("SARS-CoV-2 – Mensch PPI (Gordon et al. 2020)")
ax.legend(handles=[
    mpatches.Patch(color="#d62728", label="viral (Größe ∝ Grad)"),
    mpatches.Patch(color="#aec7e8", label="human"),
], loc="upper right")
ax.axis("off"); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
# Subnetzwerk: humane Multi-Bait-Hubs + ihre viralen Partner
hub_nodes = [n for n, _ in hubs]
H = G.subgraph(set(hub_nodes) | set(viral)).copy()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 8))
pos2 = nx.spring_layout(H, seed=7, k=0.8)
vn = [n for n in viral if n in H]
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(H, pos2, nodelist=vn,    node_color="#d62728", node_size=500, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(H, pos2, nodelist=hub_nodes, node_color="#1f77b4", node_size=200, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_edges(H, pos2, alpha=0.5, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(H, pos2, font_size=8, ax=ax)
ax.set_title("Humane Multi-Bait-Hubs und ihre viralen Partner")
ax.axis("off"); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## 2. CORDITE – Drug-Target-Netzwerk

CORDITE (Heider Lab) aggregiert publizierte Drug–Coronavirus-Protein-Interaktionen.
Hier kodiert nach CORDITE v1.0 (http://corona.mathematik.uni-marburg.de),
gefiltert auf SARS-CoV-2 und experimentell bestätigte oder klinisch relevante Einträge.

```{python}
# CORDITE-Daten: drug, target (virales Protein), typ, quelle
cordite_data = [
    # Mpro / 3CLpro (nsp5)
    ("Lopinavir",       "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Liu2020"),
    ("Ritonavir",       "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Liu2020"),
    ("Nelfinavir",      "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Nutho2020"),
    ("Saquinavir",      "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Nutho2020"),
    ("Indinavir",       "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Nutho2020"),
    ("Darunavir",       "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Nutho2020"),
    ("Boceprevir",      "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Rathnayake2020"),
    ("GC376",           "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Rathnayake2020"),
    ("Carmofur",        "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Jin2020"),
    ("Ebselen",         "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Jin2020"),
    ("Disulfiram",      "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Jin2020"),
    ("Tideglusib",      "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Jin2020"),
    ("Shikonin",        "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Jin2020"),
    ("PX-12",           "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Jin2020"),
    ("Baicalein",       "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Su2020"),
    ("Baicalin",        "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Su2020"),
    ("Quercetin",       "Mpro",  "Protease-Inhibitor", "Su2020"),
    # PLpro (nsp3)
    ("GRL0617",         "PLpro", "Deubiquitinase-Inh.", "Freitas2020"),
    ("Lopinavir",       "PLpro", "Protease-Inhibitor",  "Chou2008"),
    ("Ritonavir",       "PLpro", "Protease-Inhibitor",  "Chou2008"),
    ("Disulfiram",      "PLpro", "Cysteine-Modifier",   "Lin2018"),
    # RdRp (nsp12)
    ("Remdesivir",      "RdRp",  "Nukleosidanalogon",   "Wang2020"),
    ("Favipiravir",     "RdRp",  "Nukleosidanalogon",   "Cai2020"),
    ("Ribavirin",       "RdRp",  "Nukleosidanalogon",   "Gordon2020b"),
    ("Sofosbuvir",      "RdRp",  "Nukleosidanalogon",   "Chien2020"),
    ("Galidesivir",     "RdRp",  "Nukleosidanalogon",   "Warren2016"),
    ("EIDD-2801",       "RdRp",  "Nukleosidanalogon",   "Sheahan2020"),
    ("Tenofovir",       "RdRp",  "Nukleosidanalogon",   "Chien2020"),
    # Helicase (nsp13)
    ("SSYA10-001",      "Helicase","Helicase-Inhibitor","Adedeji2012"),
    ("Bananin",         "Helicase","Helicase-Inhibitor","Tanner2005"),
    ("Itraconazole",    "Helicase","Helicase-Inhibitor","Xu2020"),
    # Spike / Eintritt
    ("Hydroxychloroquine","Spike","Eintrittsinhibitor", "Liu2020b"),
    ("Chloroquine",     "Spike", "Eintrittsinhibitor",  "Wang2020b"),
    ("Camostat",        "Spike", "TMPRSS2-Inhibitor",   "Hoffmann2020"),
    ("Nafamostat",      "Spike", "TMPRSS2-Inhibitor",   "Hoffmann2020"),
    # Sigma-Rezeptoren (Wirtsfaktor, Nsp6/Orf7b)
    ("Haloperidol",     "SIGMAR1","Sigma-Ligand",       "Gordon2020"),
    ("Clemastine",      "SIGMAR1","Sigma-Ligand",       "Gordon2020"),
    ("Cloperastine",    "SIGMAR1","Sigma-Ligand",       "Gordon2020"),
    ("PB28",            "SIGMAR1","Sigma-Ligand",       "Gordon2020"),
    # mTOR / Translation (Wirtsfaktor)
    ("Plitidepsin",     "eEF1A", "Translationsinh.",    "White2021"),
    ("Rapamycin",       "mTOR",  "mTOR-Inhibitor",      "Stukalov2021"),
    ("Everolimus",      "mTOR",  "mTOR-Inhibitor",      "Stukalov2021"),
    # BRD (Wirtsfaktor, E-Protein)
    ("JQ1",             "BRD4",  "BET-Inhibitor",       "Gordon2020"),
    ("I-BET151",        "BRD2",  "BET-Inhibitor",       "Gordon2020"),
]

cordite = pd.DataFrame(cordite_data, columns=["drug","target","type","source"])
print(f"CORDITE-Einträge: {len(cordite)}")
print(cordite["target"].value_counts())
```

```{python}
top_targets = cordite["target"].value_counts().head(8).index.tolist()
sub = cordite[cordite["target"].isin(top_targets)]

DG = nx.Graph()
for _, r in sub.iterrows():
    DG.add_node(r["target"], kind="target")
    DG.add_node(r["drug"],   kind="drug")
    DG.add_edge(r["target"], r["drug"])

targets = [n for n, d in DG.nodes(data=True) if d.get("kind") == "target"]
drugs   = [n for n, d in DG.nodes(data=True) if d.get("kind") == "drug"]

# bipartites Layout: Targets links, Drugs rechts
pos_dt = {}
for i, t in enumerate(sorted(targets)):
    pos_dt[t] = (0, i * 2)
for i, d in enumerate(sorted(drugs)):
    pos_dt[d] = (3, i * 0.9)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 9))
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(DG, pos_dt, nodelist=targets, node_color="#2ca02c",
                       node_size=[300 + 80 * DG.degree(n) for n in targets], ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(DG, pos_dt, nodelist=drugs, node_color="#ff7f0e",
                       node_size=60, alpha=0.85, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_edges(DG, pos_dt, alpha=0.3, width=0.8, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(DG, pos_dt, labels={n: n for n in targets},
                        font_size=9, font_weight="bold", ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(DG, pos_dt, labels={n: n for n in drugs},
                        font_size=7, ax=ax)
ax.set_title("CORDITE: Drug – viraler/Wirts-Target (SARS-CoV-2, Top-8-Targets)")
ax.legend(handles=[
    mpatches.Patch(color="#2ca02c", label="Target (Größe ∝ #Drugs)"),
    mpatches.Patch(color="#ff7f0e", label="Drug"),
], loc="upper right")
ax.axis("off"); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
# Überlapp: welche Drugs treffen mehrere Targets?
multi = cordite.groupby("drug")["target"].nunique().sort_values(ascending=False)
print("Drugs mit mehreren Targets:\n", multi[multi > 1])
```

## 3. Analyse und Diskussion

### Gordon-Netzwerk

- **Orf8** ist der virale Hub mit den meisten humanen Interaktoren (~20):
  viele MHC-I-Moleküle (HLA-A/B/C/E/F) und ER-Qualitätskontroll-Proteine.
  Das deutet auf aktive **Immun-Evasion**: Orf8 stört die Antigen­präsentation.
- **N-Protein** interagiert massiv mit RNA-Bindungsproteinen (G3BP1/2, PABPC,
  HNRNP-Familie): Hijacking der Stress-Granula und Translation.
- **Nsp13 (Helicase)** trifft TBK1, einen zentralen Knoten der angeborenen
  Immunantwort (IFN-Signalweg) → Immun-Suppression.
- **Nsp6/Orf7b** binden SIGMAR1/2 (Sigma-Rezeptoren) -> direkte Verbindung zu
  den Drug-Repurposing-Kandidaten in CORDITE.
- **Humane Multi-Bait-Hubs** (z.B. SIGMAR1, PABPC1, VPS39) binden mehrere
  virale Proteine gleichzeitig, also höchstpriorisierte Drug-Targets.

### CORDITE-Netzwerk

- **Mpro** hat die meisten Inhibitoren (17) ->  intensivste Forschung,
  da die Protease essentiell und humanspezifisch ist.
- **RdRp** ist das zweite Hauptziel (Remdesivir, Favipiravir, Molnupiravir).
- **Lopinavir/Ritonavir und Disulfiram** treffen sowohl Mpro als auch PLpro
  mit einem Multi-Target-Profil, das Resistenzentwicklung erschwert.
- **Spike/TMPRSS2**: Camostat und Nafamostat blockieren den Eintritt direkt
  über TMPRSS2-Hemmung: unabhängig vom viralen Proteom.

### Verknüpfung beider Netzwerke

Die SIGMAR1-Knoten erscheinen in beiden: Gordon et al. zeigen die
virale Bindung (Nsp6, Orf7b), CORDITE liefert die Drug-Kandidaten
(Haloperidol, Clemastine). Das ist das Kernprinzip von CoVex: PPI +
Drug-Target → priorisierte Repurposing-Liste.

## Fazit

CoVex und CORDITE machen Virus-Wirt-Netzwerke und Drug-Repurposing-Analysen
zugänglich. Die zugrundeliegende Hypothese, dass zugelassene Medikamente,
die kritische Wirtsfaktoren treffen, schnell verfügbar sein könnten,
wurde während der Pandemie nur teilweise bestätigt: Dexamethason und Remdesivir erwiesen sich als wirksam, Hydroxychloroquin und Lopinavir/Ritonavir dagegen nicht.
Dennoch sind diese Netzwerke ein zentraler Baustein moderner
systembiologischer Pandemie-Antworten.

## Quellen

- Gordon D.E. et al., *A SARS-CoV-2 protein interaction map reveals targets for drug repurposing*, Nature 583, 459–468 (2020).
- Sadegh S. et al., *Exploring the SARS-CoV-2 virus-host-drug interactome for drug repurposing* (CoVex), Nat Commun 11, 3518 (2020).
- Jin Z. et al., *Structure of Mpro from SARS-CoV-2 and discovery of its inhibitors*, Nature 582, 289–293 (2020).
- Hoffmann M. et al., *SARS-CoV-2 Cell Entry Depends on ACE2 and TMPRSS2*, Cell 181, 271–280 (2020).
- CORDITE: http://corona.mathematik.uni-marburg.de
- CoVex: https://exbio.wzw.tum.de/covex/
